message:
fix(core,mesh,sandbox,store,graphql): length-prefixed HRW hash, lifecycle corrections, peer cleanup, CIDR bypass, fuel defense, TOCTOU repair

checkpoint.rs:
- Replace delimiter-based field concatenation in hrw_hash_checkpoint
  with u32 LE length-prefixed fields, preventing zone_id/epoch/node_id
  boundary ambiguity that could produce hash collisions between different
  input combinations. Update golden-vector test to compute expected hash
  dynamically since the hash algorithm changed.

lifecycle.rs:
- Fix can_start_canary: Pending cannot transition directly to Canary
  (must go through Installing first), but Production and Disabled can.
  This aligns the predicate with validate_transition's actual rules.
- Add latency_samples field to HealthMetrics, tracked separately from
  total samples, so avg_latency_ms divides by the count of observations
  that actually included latency data. Previously, health updates
  without latency (latency=None) inflated the divisor, deflating the
  average. avg_latency_ms now returns None when no latency data exists.

mesh/node.rs:
- Clean up session and admission authentication state when removing a
  peer, preventing stale auth from surviving peer removal and allowing
  a re-joining peer to bypass admission checks.

mesh/symbol_request.rs:
- Remove off-by-one saturating_add(1) on max_response_symbols limit
  that caused one extra symbol to be selected/returned beyond what the
  requester asked for. Fix was_bounded detection to also trigger when
  available symbols exactly fill the limit (more may exist upstream).

sandbox/egress.rs:
- Normalize IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:x.x.x.x) to pure IPv4
  before checking CIDR deny rules, preventing bypass of IPv4 deny
  entries via mapped-address encoding.

sandbox/wasi.rs:
- Return fuel=1 (minimal execution) instead of fuel=0 (unlimited)
  when cpu_percent=0, as defense-in-depth against misconfiguration
  granting unlimited CPU to a sandboxed module.

store/repair.rs:
- Fix TOCTOU race in next_repair: acquire the queue write lock before
  checking emptiness and consuming a rate-limit token, preventing token
  waste when the queue is drained between the two operations.

graphql/subscription.rs:
- Fix test comment: None fields do serialize as null (no
  skip_serializing_if on GraphqlWsMessage).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
